title: Festuca debilis
taxonomy: Festuca debilis is a species of grass in the family Poaceae. It belongs to the genus Festuca, which includes various species of fescue grasses. Festuca debilis is further classified as a member of the tribe Poeae and the subfamily Pooideae. The plant's classification is supported by its morphological characteristics and genetic studies. Festuca debilis is a type of perennial grass native to mountainous regions.

intro: Festuca debilis, also known as weak fescue, is a type of grass that belongs to the family Poaceae. This plant is valued for its potential health benefits, which include anti-inflammatory, antioxidant, and antimicrobial properties, making it a possible remedy for various ailments such as wound healing, fever reduction, and digestive issues. The bioactive constituents of Festuca debilis include flavonoids, phenolic acids, and terpenoids, which contribute to its medicinal properties.
